title: BITSAT 2021
question: Neeraj starts walking towards South. After walking 15 m, he turns towards North. After walking 20 m, the turns towards East and walks 10 m. He then turns towards South and walks 5 m. How far is he from his original position and in which direction?
solution:

title: BITSAT 2021
question: In a row of forty children, P is thirteenth from the left end and Q is ninth from the right end. How many children are there between P and R, if R is fourth to the left of Q?
solution:
According to the question

From left end, position of R = Total children − Position from right end +1 = 40 − 13 + 1 = 28 th

Hence, number of children between P and R = (28 - 13) - 1 = 14.

title: BITSAT 2023

Who is sitting between $$\mathrm{A}$$ and $$\mathrm{F}$$ ?

title: BITSAT 2023
question: Two different positions of the same dice are shown below. Select the number that will be at the top if ' 6 ' is at the bottom.
solution: Here, 1 is common and it is on the same face in both the positions. So, 4 and 2 are opposite, 6 and 3 are opposite. Hence, 3 will be at the top if 6 is at the bottom.

title: BITSAT 2024
question: In a row all the women are facing South, Monika is 29 th from the left end and in the right side of Monika, there are only 19 women. Find the total number of women in this queue?
solution:
According to the question,

$ \Rightarrow $ Total women in the row $ =29+19=48 $

Alternate Method

Total number of women

$ = $ Position of Monika from the one end + Number of women on her right side

$ =29+19=48 $

title: BITSAT 2025
question: Stanley starts cycling from point $A$. He cycles $x$ km towards the West and then turns right and cycles 3 km . He again turns right and cycles for $x+1 \mathrm{~km}$. He again turns right and cycles for $2 x+1 \mathrm{~km}$. He takes a final right turn and cycles for 1 km to stop at point $B$. Point $B$ is 6 km South of point $\boldsymbol{A}$. What is the value of $x$ ?
solution:
$$ \text { As per the question, } $$

From the above diagram,

$$ \begin{array}{rlrl} & 6+3=9 =2 x+1 \\ \Rightarrow & 9-1 =2 x \\ \Rightarrow & 8 =2 x \\ \Rightarrow & x =4 \end{array} $$

Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.
